description:
No spec subject — repo/CI configuration.

Observed 2026-07-22 shipping PR #3: neither the pull_request event (specs.yml has matching path filters) nor the push-to-main event triggered a Specs run; the repo's ENTIRE Actions history is workflow_dispatch only. All 4 CI runs for the 0.2.0 ship were manually dispatched. Until fixed, merges get zero automatic CI.

Check: repo Settings -> Actions (workflow permissions / allowed events), org-level Actions policy, and whether the workflow file on the default branch differs from expectations. Acceptance: opening a PR that touches lib/** produces an automatic Specs run.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: specled_-dkh-ci-event-triggered-github (interactive implement session)
Log: interactive session, no delegated log file

Commit: 8e30724 (shared acceptance probe = PR #4 on branch specled_-y1c/ci-pr-base)
Files touched: .github/workflows/specs.yml (the y1c change; serves as this ticket's probe)
Tests added: none (CI/repo configuration ticket)
Verification stubs flipped: none (no spec subject)
Verification tier: live acceptance probe — opening PR #4 (touches .github/workflows/**, a filtered path) produced automatic pull_request-triggered Specs run 29968310586, the first event-triggered run in the repo's Actions history; run completed green after one rerun of the known-flaky tracer_test.exs:241 failure (tracked as specled_-epl, unrelated)
Final-tier runs: 1 (plus 1 rerun for the pre-existing specled_-epl flake)

Summary: Diagnosis ruled out every configurable cause: workflow file on origin/main has correct pull_request/push triggers with matching path filters (PR #3 touched lib/**, .spec/**, mix.exs), workflow state=active, Actions enabled with allowed_actions=all, and GitHub received the PushEvent/PullRequestEvent for PR #3 without creating runs. Root cause is the forked-repo workflow-registration state: runs are not created retroactively for PRs opened before the fork's workflows were (re)enabled — PR #3 predated that. Acceptance now observed: new PRs trigger automatically. Push-to-main leg to be confirmed when PR #4 merges.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: delegated cold-verify agent (no uuid)
Log: delegated agent, no log path

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 1 (.github/workflows/specs.yml)
- Files outside Allowed globs: n/a — ticket defines NO Allowed-touches / Out-of-Scope (files) / Out-of-Scope (intent) sections (confirmed absent in bw JSON). The single touched file is the shared y1c change (commit 8e30724, 'run spec.check against PR merge base'), bundled on stage branch specled_-y1c/ci-pr-base and used as this ticket's live PR probe. dkh itself contributes zero code — its fix is a repo/fork workflow-registration state change, not a diff.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: n/a (no globs defined)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0, passed: 0 (no spec subject — repo/CI configuration)
- Requirements coverage (acceptance criterion):
  - 'Opening a PR that touches filtered paths produces an automatic Specs run': COVERED. PR #4 (iautom8things/specled_ex, OPEN, head specled_-y1c/ci-pr-base -> main) touches .github/workflows/specs.yml, which matches on.pull_request.paths filter '.github/workflows/**'. That open produced workflow run 29968310586: event=pull_request, headSha=8e30724, job 'Check Specs'=success, conclusion=success. This is the FIRST pull_request-event run in the repo's entire Actions history — every prior Specs run (29945270272, 29944245057, 29944042016, 29943626118, 28630010862, 28622095868, 28620073734) is event=workflow_dispatch; the only other non-dispatch runs are 'pages build and deployment' (event=dynamic, gh-pages), not the Specs workflow. Would fail if: run 29968310586 had event!=pull_request, or no pull_request run existed for the head branch, or prior history already contained pull_request runs (i.e. nothing changed). None hold. Seams: none.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. Diff is purely the CI merge-base spec.check change (fetch-depth: 0 + pull_request-conditional --base origin/$GITHUB_BASE_REF); nothing unrelated.
- Targeted verification: PASS. Read-only gh api only (pr view, run list, run view). No GitHub state mutated — no reruns, dispatches, or enable/disable.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The acceptance is proven by external GitHub state (the live pull_request run), not by the diff — appropriate for a fork-registration fix that has no code deliverable of its own.
- Original ticket text phrased acceptance as 'touches lib/**'; the probe touched '.github/workflows/**' instead. Both are equally in on.pull_request.paths, so the probe is valid; the general acceptance is 'filtered paths', which is satisfied.
- Push-to-main leg is NOT yet confirmed (PR #4 unmerged); implementer disclosed this. Not required by the ticket's 'opening a PR' acceptance — will self-confirm on merge.
- Run 29968310586 shows conclusion=success directly (no failing job); the implementer's mention of a tracer_test flake rerun (specled_-epl) is not contradicted by current state and is out of scope here.

AUDIT: APPROVE

Session: cold-audit agent (delegated, no uuid)
Log: delegated agent, no log path

Reasoning: The acceptance ("opening a PR that touches filtered paths produces an automatic Specs run") is proven by independently re-read GitHub state, not by a claim. `gh run view 29968310586` returns event=pull_request, headBranch=specled_-y1c/ci-pr-base, headSha=8e30724, workflowName=Specs, conclusion=success — a genuine event-triggered run, not a workflow_dispatch passed off as one. `gh run list --limit 30` confirms it is the ONLY pull_request Specs run in the repo's entire history; every other Specs run (29945270272, 29944245057, 29944042016, 29943626118, 28630010862, 28622095868, 28620073734) is event=workflow_dispatch, and the only other non-dispatch runs are pages-build-deployment (event=dynamic, gh-pages). The path-filter premise is real: specs.yml:4-11 lists `.github/workflows/**` under on.pull_request.paths, and PR #4 touches only .github/workflows/specs.yml — so the run firing genuinely exercises the filter (a PR touching an unfiltered path would not trigger). The verifier's falsifiability is real and load-bearing: had the run been workflow_dispatch, absent, or had prior pull_request runs existed, acceptance fails; none hold.

Mechanical re-check: `git diff 966b28e..HEAD` touches exactly 1 file (.github/workflows/specs.yml), matching the verifier's count; the diff is purely the y1c CI change (fetch-depth: 0 + pull_request-conditional --base "origin/${GITHUB_BASE_REF}"). `gh pr view 4` confirms PR #4 OPEN, head specled_-y1c/ci-pr-base -> main, one file modified (+11/-1). Scope at intent level: the ticket has no Allowed-touches/Out-of-Scope sections, but the entire diff is the CI gate change owned by y1c — dkh contributing zero code of its own is by design (its deliverable is diagnosis + live acceptance probe). No scope creep.

The two Notes are honest disclosures, not downgraded contradictions: (1) the push-to-main leg is genuinely not required by an "opening a PR" acceptance and is correctly deferred to merge; (2) the probe touching .github/workflows/** rather than lib/** is immaterial since both are equally in on.pull_request.paths. No adjacent-behavior substitution, no non-falsifiable claims, no scope creep.
APPROVED. No stage merge-back (standalone ticket, no epic); fix lands via PR #4. Closing per auditor sign-off.

Post-close confirmation: PR #4 merge (b00e02b) automatically triggered a push-event Specs run on main at 2026-07-23T00:22:32Z, completed success. Both pull_request and push legs of the acceptance now observed.

Fresh evidence from the 0.3.0 ship (2026-07-23): push event DID fire for main@b00e02b (run 29968890502, event=push, created 2026-07-23T00:22Z) but did NOT fire for main@028fa5a (the #5 squash-merge, 08:42Z) — nothing after 60s polling; had to workflow_dispatch (run 29992077790). pull_request events also never fired on PR #5. So event delivery is intermittent, not uniformly dead — one push run exists.

title: CI: event-triggered GitHub Actions never fire (pull_request/push) — every run is manual workflow_dispatch
